arts.ttl: Milan v Liverpool: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: As Milan prepare for their Champions League opener against Liverpool, manager Paulo Fonseca faces a crucial decision regarding his starting lineup. The match, set to be a litmus test for both teams’ ambitions this season, comes on the heels of Milan’s confidence-boosting victory over Venezia and precedes the highly anticipated derby against Inter Milan. Fonseca’s starting eleven is nearly settled, with only a few positions still in question. In attack, Alvaro Morata is set to return, relegating Tammy Abraham to the bench. arts.ttl: 'It's beneficial for Chelsea, Arsenal, Liverpool, Man Utd for Man City to look bad': City expert reacts to 115 charges hearing
arts.sum: Man City expert Steven McInerney from Esteemed Kompany admits to Sports Mole that it is beneficial for Premier League clubs including Chelsea, Arsenal, Liverpool and Man United for the Citizens to "look bad" amidst the 115 charges against them.

arts.ttl: The Miracle of Istanbul: Jerzy Dudek reflects on Liverpool’s legendary Champions League triumph
arts.sum: Former Liverpool goalkeeper Jerzy Dudek shared his memories of Liverpool’s miraculous Champions League victory against Milan. The one-time Polish international sp0ke to La Gazzetta dello Sport about the 2005 final against AC Milan, a match that has become etched in football lore. Liverpool found themselves in a dire situation at halftime, trailing 3-0 to a star-studded Milan side. Dudek recalls the turning point: “The comeback began in the dressing room.
